title: Commit host-independent persistent ID namespace (w3id.org/PURL)
context: The corpus must use persistent, host-independent identifiers so that place and recording IRIs survive a hosting migration (the steward is not yet secured). The ID namespace must be registered with w3id.org or a comparable PURL service, decoupled from any GitHub URL or as-yet-unconfirmed domain. Every Place, Recording, ConsentRecord, and OriginNote entity will carry this stable prefix. This task depends on place-name-pronunciation-schema-001 having defined the record types that need IDs.
objective: Register and document a stable, host-independent ID namespace for the corpus (w3id.org/place-name-pronunciation or equivalent PURL), specify the minting pattern for each record type (Place, Recording, Speaker, ConsentRecord, OriginNote), and commit an ID-NAMESPACE.md document describing the namespace, pattern, and resolution intent.
acceptanceCriteria: A stable namespace prefix is registered with w3id.org or an equivalent PURL service and is documented in ID-NAMESPACE.md.
The ID minting pattern for each record type (Place, Recording, Speaker, ConsentRecord, OriginNote) is defined with examples.
The namespace is decoupled from GitHub URLs and the not-yet-secured hosting domain.
ID-NAMESPACE.md records the registration method, resolution intent, and the process for future prefix changes.
The chosen prefix is reflected as a constant or config value in the schema package so all tooling uses it consistently.
Maintainer reviews and approves the namespace commitment before the task is marked done.
output: ID-NAMESPACE.md committed via PR documenting the registered namespace, minting patterns per record type, and resolution intent.
